source: User requested assurance that code/docs/intent changes propagate through CodeWiki; accepted fixes after current-state review.
summary: Drift propagation assurance gaps accepted for implementation.

diff_table.current_state: Graph reconciliation can report aligned while status reports untracked wiki drift.
diff_table.desired_state: Graph, status, and codewiki_state share one reconciliation truth or explicitly expose separate lint/status drift without contradiction.
diff_table.rationale: A core drift product cannot give conflicting next-action signals.

diff_table.current_state: Deterministic lint drift uses hardcoded thresholds and can ignore project config.
diff_table.desired_state: Lint drift honors configured thresholds/exemptions while preserving cheap deterministic checks.
diff_table.rationale: False drift wastes user/agent attention and token budget.

diff_table.current_state: Freshness anchors can reset on each rebuild because previous status is read from the graph container, not the nested status lens.
diff_table.desired_state: Freshness compares current lane anchors against the previous status lens and only marks stale when actual spec/task/code revisions change.
diff_table.rationale: Freshness over time is the assurance mechanism for ongoing coherence.

diff_table.current_state: Tests cover build/validation reconciliation, but not enough graph/status agreement and freshness behavior.
diff_table.desired_state: Targeted smoke/feature tests prove config-driven lint, consistent drift routing, and stable freshness anchors without broad context dumps.
diff_table.rationale: Assurance requires mechanical proof, not chat confidence.

accepted_decisions.summary: Implement assurance-grade drift propagation fixes while keeping generated graph compact and route-oriented.
Prefer reuse of existing graph/status lenses over new long-form caches.
Add targeted tests rather than expanding default codewiki_state output.

assumptions: Current user approval covers documentation, roadmap, code, and tests needed for these fixes.
non_goals: No large context dumps in status/state output.
No broad refactor of compiler or agency architecture.

knowledge: Clarify graph freshness/drift consistency invariants if needed.
roadmap: Create one scoped task for assurance work.
code: Fix lint config use, status/graph drift selection, freshness anchor reading, and tests.

knowledge: Clarify graph freshness/drift consistency invariants if needed.
roadmap: Create one scoped task for assurance work.
code: Fix lint config use, status/graph drift selection, freshness anchor reading, and tests.
